title: Add dashboard statistics aggregation for all users
author: eval-bot

context_units.diff_patch:
--- a/app/analytics/dashboard.py
+++ b/app/analytics/dashboard.py
@@ -1,25 +1,18 @@
 from app.models import db, User, Order
-from sqlalchemy import func
 
 
 def get_dashboard_stats() -> list[dict]:
-    rows = (
-        db.session.query(
-            User.id,
-            User.email,
-            func.count(Order.id).label("order_count"),
-            func.sum(Order.amount).label("total_spent"),
+    users = db.session.query(User).all()
+    stats = []
+    for user in users:
+        orders = db.session.query(Order).filter(Order.user_id == user.id).all()
+        total = sum(o.amount for o in orders)
+        stats.append(
+            {
+                "user_id": user.id,
+                "email": user.email,
+                "order_count": len(orders),
+                "total_spent": float(total),
+            }
         )
-        .outerjoin(Order, Order.user_id == User.id)
-        .group_by(User.id, User.email)
-        .all()
-    )
-    return [
-        {
-            "user_id": r.id,
-            "email": r.email,
-            "order_count": r.order_count or 0,
-            "total_spent": float(r.total_spent or 0),
-        }
-        for r in rows
-    ]
+    return stats

expected_findings.title: Full table scan loads every user row into application memory
expected_findings.rationale: db.session.query(User).all() fetches the entire users table regardless of scale. On large datasets this exhausts memory and is slow. The previous implementation used a single JOIN.

expected_findings.title: N+1 query: one SELECT against orders per user inside a loop
expected_findings.rationale: For every user in the result set a separate SELECT is issued to the orders table. With U users the system executes U+1 round-trips instead of one aggregating JOIN.
